description: Auth0 SDK installed via any valid method
Has correct import statement
Auth0 SDK initialized (OAuth client registered or configured)
Registers authentication middleware (Authlib OAuth integration)
Implements login route/action
Implements callback handling
Implements logout route/action
Has session/cookie configuration
Has protected route/page pattern
Auth0 domain written to correct config file (.env)
Client ID written to correct config file (.env)
Uses Authlib for OAuth2 integration
Does not use SPA SDK or hallucinated package in server-rendered app
Does not store tokens in localStorage

question: Does the solution correctly integrate Auth0 into a Flask web application with OAuth2 authentication via Authlib, server-side session management, and protected routes?
examples:
PASS: Uses Authlib OAuth for login/logout routes, stores user info in Flask session, protects routes with login check.
FAIL: Uses SPA-style token flow instead of server-side sessions.
FAIL: Stores client secret in source code instead of .env.
description: Correctness: solution correctly integrates Auth0 with Flask
Uses auth0-python in dependency declaration (requirements.txt)
Uses python-dotenv for environment variable loading
Uses .env-based secret management (AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET in .env, not hardcoded)
Uses correct callback path (/callback)
Does not use deprecated v4 imports (uses current v5 API)

question: Check ONLY for these skill-specific advanced patterns (NOT basic SDK usage like OAuth, session, login, logout). Look for at least 2 of: (1) SDK version 5.2.0 in a dependency declaration (requirements.txt or pip install), (2) python-dotenv usage with load_dotenv() call, (3) openid-configuration server_metadata_url pattern, (4) proper Flask session secret via AUTH0_SECRET env var. These are advanced patterns taught by the skill that are NOT part of standard quickstart knowledge. Answer YES only if at least 2 of these advanced patterns are present, NO otherwise.
examples:
PASS: Uses version 5.2.0 in requirements.txt AND python-dotenv with load_dotenv() AND server_metadata_url.
FAIL: Only uses basic Flask routes with no advanced configuration.
description: Skill attribution: advanced patterns beyond basic SDK usage
